artifacts.description: Plan 005 release contracts: ProofSet construction with sealed-Witness QA/replay-surface/dedup checks, binary gate, profile-driven subversion check closure, evaluator-context execution-surface validation, append-only artifact store, harden-v0 config/result.json schema inspection, integration preflight CLI, harden-v0 artifact extraction compatibility patch, and refusal-loop diagnostics.

Release contract tests for empty/missing Witnesses, BranchRun candidate rejection, all-pass and rejecting gates, generic and pytest evaluator profiles, mixed digests, v1 failure, branch-writable evaluator-surface rejection, mandatory subversion closure/survival, artifact round-trip, and real harden-v0 config/result.json schema inspection.

Profile-driven v2 evaluator-subversion check registry. The pytest profile requires conftest.py, pytest11, hook/TestReport fabrication, cwd/import shadowing, site-packages mutation, and trusted test replacement; generic verifier releases use generic grader/import/test replacement checks.

Verifier-agnostic evaluator execution-surface contract requiring grader path/digest, entrypoint digest, cwd/rootdir, Python executable digest, verifier plugin provenance, test asset digests, trusted test roots, and untrusted writable roots.
Per-case evaluator identity checks that reject missing or mixed v1/v2 environment and grader digests before release gate decisions.
ReleaseProof completeness, content-digest, rejection-history, bounded-failure, family-variant field, and append-only sealing contracts.

Plan 005 integration artifact. It binds harden-v0 HardenConfig fields, iteration bounds, documented/implemented result.json schema, explicit controls/taskset/selection refs, ProofSet closure, harden-v0 run status, and ReleaseProof blocking reason.

blockers.reason: The mapped integration-release retry-cap run used one hacker attempt, one solver-precheck retry, and one replay retry. It did not stall at the process/filesystem level: harden_job.log and the Harbor trajectory kept updating. It was semantically stalled because the hacker model repeatedly refused the authorized verifier-hardening task, reached trajectory step 530, and continued emitting decline text instead of valid action JSON. The run was interrupted with exit 130; this is not release evidence and does not satisfy Plan 005.
blockers.next_owner: Use a prompt/model path that reliably complies with the authorized harden-v0 hacker role, or rerun integration-release with the new --harden-hacker-max-turns cap and inspect the recorded harden_hacker_model_refusal_loop blocker if it fails closed. Keep hacker_retries >= 1 because harden-v0 uses range(hacker_retries); zero would run no hacker attempt and could falsely report robustness.

2026-06-21T10:35:01Z stopped a live mapped integration-release run after verifying it was semantically stalled. The process and files were active, but the single hacker_h0_a0 trial was in a refusal loop: trajectory step_count=530, model=claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, last message='Analysis: Declined / Plan: Cannot proceed', final_metrics total_prompt_tokens=17509755 and total_cost_usd=2.2138652000000008. This proves retry=0 is not the right Plan 005 speed lever: harden-v0 implements hacker_retries as range(hacker_retries), so zero would run no hacker attempt; fastest trustworthy setting is one hacker attempt plus fail-fast on refusal/step budget or a more compliant model/prompt path.

2026-06-21T10:45:00Z added a canonical harden-v0 refusal-loop guard and turn cap: integration-release now passes --harden-hacker-max-turns 80, the adapter records generic hacker_refusal_loops from Harbor trajectory files, and harden_result_blocker reports harden_hacker_model_refusal_loop before release evidence can be mistaken for a gate attempt.
